title: JulesSession
description: Ontology node tracking a Jules-orchestrator session through its lifecycle. The `state` field is the canonical state-machine label written by the row's six orchestration handlers (dispatch, await_plan, monitor, verify, recover, integrate).

session_id.description: Opaque Jules session identifier (numeric, as returned by the v1alpha API). Stored without the `sessions/` prefix.

state.description: Local lifecycle label. Distinct from Jules's own state field (which only tracks the API session); the local label additionally records verify / recover / integrate phase outcomes.

jules_state.description: Last observed value of the Jules API's `state` field — `IN_PROGRESS`, `AWAITING_PLAN_APPROVAL`, `COMPLETED`, `FAILED`, etc. Distinct from the `state` field above which carries local lifecycle labels.

title.description: Human-readable session title (echoed back from the dispatch prompt or set explicitly).

owner.description: GitHub owner (org / user) the session targets.

repo.description: GitHub repository name the session targets.

branch.description: Starting branch the session was based on.

prompt.description: The natural-language task brief sent to Jules.

url.description: Jules web UI URL for the session.

created_at.description: Unix epoch seconds when the JulesSession node was first written (i.e. when phase 03 dispatch succeeded).

plan_approved_at.description: Unix epoch when phase 04 await_plan called jules_approve. Null until then.

completed_at.description: Unix epoch when monitor first observed jules_state=COMPLETED.

verified_at.description: Unix epoch when verify confirmed the work landed (state=VERIFIED) or flagged silent-fail (state=SILENT_FAIL).

branch_on_remote.description: Verify-phase result of the JULES_PROTOCOL §8 check — does origin carry the session's expected work branch? Null until verify runs.

pr_url.description: Pull-request URL once integrate phase has surfaced one (either auto-created by Jules or opened by recover).

patch_bytes.description: Size of the patch body extracted by recover, when the session went through silent-fail.

patch_files.description: Count of files touched by the patch, when the session went through silent-fail.

last_error.description: Most-recent error message stamped onto the session by any orchestration handler. Cleared on the next successful transition.
